message:
feat(tui): timeline splitter handles, config-path presets, and thread drag/drop edge-case tests

Timeline screen (tui_screens/timeline.rs):
- Add render_splitter_handle() for visual pane separation between the
  primary timeline list and the dock/inspector panel, with themed
  mid-dot indicator when dock drag is active.
- Introduce TIMELINE_SPLIT_GAP_THRESHOLD (60 cols/rows) to gate the
  gap rendering based on available terminal extent.
- Refactor view() to compute adjusted primary_area and detail_area
  accounting for the splitter gap in all four dock positions (Right,
  Left, Bottom, Top), ensuring the gap never consumes the dock area
  when terminal is too small.
- Refactor build() to accept filter_presets_path_override parameter,
  enabling with_config() to derive the presets path directly from
  Config::console_persist_path instead of re-computing it. This fixes
  a subtle bug where with_config() and with_filter_presets_path_for_test()
  could disagree on the presets file location.
- Add test with_config_uses_console_persist_path_for_presets verifying
  the config-driven preset path round-trips correctly.
- Allow clippy::too_many_lines on the expanded view() method.

Threads screen (tui_screens/threads.rs):
- Add test mouse_drag_over_invalid_target_sets_warning_snapshot:
  verifies that dragging a message over an area outside any valid drop
  zone produces a drag snapshot with invalid_hover=true, hovered_is_valid=false,
  and hovered_thread_id=None.
- Add test mouse_drop_on_invalid_target_is_noop: verifies that releasing
  a drag on an invalid target returns Cmd::None and resets drag state
  to Idle without clearing the shared state snapshot.
- Add test ctrl_v_same_thread_is_noop_and_preserves_marker: verifies
  that keyboard-paste (Ctrl+V) targeting the same source thread returns
  Cmd::None and preserves the keyboard move marker for retry, rather
  than consuming and discarding it.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
